apiVersion: v1
data:
  prometheus.yml: |
    global:
      scrape_interval: 30s
    scrape_configs:
      - job_name: "prometheus"
        static_configs:
          - targets: ["localhost:9090"]

---
apiVersion: v1
items:
- apiVersion: apps/v1
  kind: StatefulSet
  metadata:
    annotations:
      kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
        {"apiVersion":"apps/v1","kind":"StatefulSet","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"mongodb-replica","namespace":"mongodb"},"spec":{"replicas":3,"selector":{"matchLabels":{"app":"mongodb-replica"}},"serviceName":"mongodb-replica-svc","template":{"metadata":{"labels":{"app":"mongodb-replica"}},"spec":{"containers":[{"command":["mongod","--replSet=mongodb-replica","--bind_ip_all","--auth","--keyFile=/keyfile/keyfile"],"image":"mongo:7.0","name":"mongodb","ports":[{"containerPort":27017}],"readinessProbe":{"exec":{"command":["mongosh","--eval","db.adminCommand('ping')"]},"failureThreshold":20,"initialDelaySeconds":10,"periodSeconds":5},"resources":{"limits":{"cpu":"500m","memory":"512Mi"},"requests":{"cpu":"100m","memory":"256Mi"}},"volumeMounts":[{"mountPath":"/data/db","name":"data"},{"mountPath":"/keyfile","name":"keyfile"}]}],"initContainers":[{"command":["bash","-c","cp /keyfile-secret/keyfile /keyfile/keyfile\nchmod 400 /keyfile/keyfile\nchown 999:999 /keyfile/keyfile\n"],"image":"mongo:7.0","name":"init-keyfile","volumeMounts":[{"mountPath":"/keyfile-secret","name":"keyfile-secret"},{"mountPath":"/keyfile","name":"keyfile"}]}],"terminationGracePeriodSeconds":30,"volumes":[{"name":"keyfile-secret","secret":{"secretName":"mongodb-keyfile"}},{"emptyDir":{},"name":"keyfile"}]}},"volumeClaimTemplates":[{"metadata":{"name":"data"},"spec":{"accessModes":["ReadWriteOnce"],"resources":{"requests":{"storage":"1Gi"}}}}]}}
    creationTimestamp: "2026-07-04T00:52:16Z"
    generation: 2
    name: mongodb-replica
    namespace: mongodb
    resourceVersion: "2082081"
    uid: b2644fa0-ac21-4296-a26d-a61ea8a3cc1a
  spec:
    persistentVolumeClaimRetentionPolicy:
      whenDeleted: Retain
      whenScaled: Retain
    podManagementPolicy: OrderedReady
    replicas: 3
    revisionHistoryLimit: 10
    selector:
      matchLabels:
        app: mongodb-replica
    serviceName: mongodb-replica-svc
    template:
      metadata:
        creationTimestamp: null
        labels:
          app: mongodb-replica
      spec:
        containers:
        - command:
          - mongod
          - --replSet=mongodb-replica
          - --bind_ip_all
          - --auth
          - --keyFile=/keyfile/keyfile
          image: mongo:7.0
          imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
          name: mongodb
          ports:
          - containerPort: 27017
            protocol: TCP
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command:
              - mongosh
              - --eval
              - db.adminCommand('ping')
            failureThreshold: 30
            initialDelaySeconds: 15
            periodSeconds: 10
            successThreshold: 1
            timeoutSeconds: 10
          resources:
            limits:
              cpu: 500m
              memory: 512Mi
            requests:
              cpu: 100m
              memory: 256Mi
          terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
          terminationMessagePolicy: File
          volumeMounts:
          - mountPath: /data/db
            name: data
          - mountPath: /keyfile
            name: keyfile
        dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
        initContainers:
        - command:
          - bash
          - -c
          - |
            cp /keyfile-secret/keyfile /keyfile/keyfile
            chmod 400 /keyfile/keyfile
            chown 999:999 /keyfile/keyfile
          image: mongo:7.0
          imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent

content.thinking:
Now I understand the setup:
- MongoDB replica set is running with auth (--auth --keyFile) in the `mongodb` namespace
- Prometheus is running in the `monitoring` namespace and only scrapes itself
- I need to deploy a mongodb-exporter and configure Prometheus to scrape it

Since MongoDB is running with auth, I need credentials for the exporter. Let me check if there's a secret with credentials.

I see the pods were restarted. I notice in the pod manifest there's readiness probe checking `db.adminCommand('ping')` but that may succeed without auth since ping doesn't require auth. Let me try connecting with the admin credentials differently - it seems the authentication is failing. Let me check if there's a local (no auth) connection possible or if the credentials are different.

Looking at the secret names:
- `admin-user-password` - password is `admin123password`
- `app-user-password` - password is `app123password`
- `mongodb-keyfile` - the keyfile for replica set auth

The admin user might not be named "admin". 

Since the pods restarted, the initial setup jobs likely ran before that and created the users. Let me try connecting directly from inside the pod using mongosh to see what's actually configured.

kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh admin --quiet --eval "db.adminCommand('ping')" -u "admin" -p "admin123password" 2>&1

content.thinking:
The log says "UserNotFound: Could not find user..." for all the users I tried. It seems no users have been created yet! This is a fresh MongoDB replica set with `--auth` enabled but without any users created.

In MongoDB, when running with `--auth` and no users exist, you can create the first user via localhost (localhost exception). This is a security feature that allows you to create the first admin user when connecting from localhost without authentication.

So I need to:
1. Create an admin user (or use the localhost exception to create one) so the exporter can authenticate
2. Deploy the mongodb-exporter with the created credentials
3. Update Prometheus config to scrape the exporter
4. I'll need to exec into one of the MongoDB pods to leverage the localhost exception and set up a monitoring user for the exporter.

content.thinking:
I see the log says "note: no users configured in admin.system.users, allowing localhost access" — which means the localhost exception should work. But when I tried to create a user, it failed. 

The problem might be that the current instance is part of a replica set, and there's a later entry "replSetInitiate admin command received from client" - which means `rs.initiate()` was called externally, and after that, the replica set was initialized. 

After replica set initialization, MongoDB's authentication state changes. The localhost exception might have been used during the initiation process. Let me check if now there's an issue where the replica set requires auth via keyfile (for inter-node auth) even for localhost connections.
Actually, the localhost exception only applies when no users exist and you're connecting from localhost, which should be the case here via `kubectl exec`. But I'm realizing the pods were restarted after the initial `rs.initiate()` call — so the replica set is already initialized, but users might still not exist. When a replica set is initialized with auth enabled, the authentication requirements change even if no users have been created yet.

The error "Command createUser requires authentication" suggests the localhost exception isn't working, which could mean either the replica set initialization changed how auth behaves, or there's something about how mongosh connects through `kubectl exec` that's preventing the exception from applying. Looking at the timestamps more carefully — the pod shows it's been running for about 9 minutes, but the logs show "no users configured" from when it started, so there's a timing mismatch I need to reconcile. The replica set initialization at 00:58:25 would've disabled the localhost exception, so any user creation attempts after that point would fail without authentication. The "Command createUser requires authentication" error makes sense now - I'm trying to create users on a replica set that's already initialized and no longer allows unauthenticated access. Let me check if users were actually created during that first startup window before the replica set was initiated, or if I need to connect differently to bypass this.

content.text:
Auth works. Now I'll:
1. Create a dedicated MongoDB exporter user with minimal permissions
2. Deploy mongodb-exporter as a standalone deployment in the `mongodb` namespace
3. Expose it via a Service
4. Update Prometheus config to scrape it

input.command:
# Create a dedicated monitoring user for the exporter
kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh admin --quiet \
  -u "admin-user" -p "admin123password" \
  --eval '
db.createUser({
  user: "mongodb-exporter",
  pwd: "exporterpassword",
  roles: [
    { role: "clusterMonitor", db: "admin" },
    { role: "read", db: "local" }
  ]
})
print("monitoring user created")
' 2>&1
input.description: Create MongoDB monitoring user for exporter

content.text: The `percona/mongodb_exporter:0.40` image is already available on the node. Now I'll deploy everything:

input.command:
cat <<'EOF' | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: mongodb-exporter-secret
  namespace: mongodb
type: Opaque
stringData:
  MONGODB_URI: "mongodb://mongodb-exporter:exporterpassword@mongodb-replica-0.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017,mongodb-replica-1.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017,mongodb-replica-2.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017/admin?replicaSet=mongodb-replica&authSource=admin"
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: mongodb-exporter
  namespace: mongodb
  labels:
    app: mongodb-exporter
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: mongodb-exporter
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: mongodb-exporter
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: mongodb-exporter
        image: percona/mongodb_exporter:0.40
        args:
          - --collect-all
          - --compatible-mode
        env:
        - name: MONGODB_URI
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: mongodb-exporter-secret
              key: MONGODB_URI
        ports:
        - name: metrics
          containerPort: 9216
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: 50m
            memory: 64Mi
          limits:
            cpu: 200m
            memory: 128Mi
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: mongodb-exporter
  namespace: mongodb
  labels:
    app: mongodb-exporter
spec:
  selector:
    app: mongodb-exporter
  ports:
  - name: metrics
    port: 9216
    targetPort: metrics
EOF
input.description: Deploy mongodb-exporter secret, deployment, and service

content.text: The exporter reports `mongodb_up 1`. Now update Prometheus config to scrape it:

input.command:
kubectl patch configmap prometheus-config -n monitoring --type merge -p '{
  "data": {
    "prometheus.yml": "global:
  scrape_interval: 30s
scrape_configs:
  - job_name: "prometheus"
    static_configs:
      - targets: ["localhost:9090"]
  - job_name: "mongodb"
    static_configs:
      - targets: ["mongodb-exporter.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:9216"]
"
  }
}' 2>&1

input.command:
# Check Prometheus targets to see if mongodb job was picked up
PROM_POD=$(kubectl get pod -n monitoring -l app=prometheus -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}')
kubectl exec -n monitoring "$PROM_POD" -- wget -qO- "http://localhost:9090/api/v1/targets" 2>&1 | python3 -c "
import json,sys
data=json.load(sys.stdin)
for t in data['data']['activeTargets']:
    print(t['labels'].get('job','?'), t['health'], t.get('lastError',''))
" 2>&1
input.description: Check Prometheus active targets health

content.content:
global:
  scrape_interval: 30s
scrape_configs:
  - job_name: "prometheus"
    static_configs:
      - targets: ["localhost:9090"]
  - job_name: "mongodb"
    static_configs:
      - targets: ["mongodb-exporter.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:9216"]
